# Memory Extended Cache Driver

The Memory Extended Cache Driver is similar to the [Memory Cache Driver](./memory), but with **sliding expiration**: every time you access a key, its TTL is reset.

## When to Use

- You want cache entries to stay alive as long as they're being used
- Session-like or frequently accessed data

## Best For

- User sessions
- Frequently accessed, but not permanent, data

## Limitations

- Data is lost on restart
- Not shared between processes or servers
- No eviction policy (see [LRU Memory Cache Driver](./lru-memory))

## Options

- `globalPrefix`: Prefix for all keys (string or function)
- `ttl`: Time to live in seconds (default: Infinity)

## Example Usage

```ts title="src/config/cache.ts"
import { MemoryExtendedCacheDriver } from "@warlock.js/core";

const cacheConfigurations = {
  drivers: {
    memoryExtended: MemoryExtendedCacheDriver,
  },
  default: "memoryExtended",
  options: {
    memoryExtended: {
      ttl: 600, // 10 minutes
    },
  },
};
```

## Storing and Retrieving Data

```ts
import { cache } from "@warlock.js/core";

await cache.set("session:abc", { userId: 1 }, 600);
const session = await cache.get("session:abc"); // TTL resets to 10 minutes
```

## Troubleshooting

- **Data disappears after restart**: This is expected. Use [File](./file) or [Redis](./redis) for persistence.
- **Cache not shared between processes**: Use [Redis](./redis) for distributed cache.
